reasoning: Based on climatological averages for Bordeaux-Mérignac, the mean daily temperature steadily drops from early November (approx 10.8°C) to mid-December (~7.7°C) and remains low through early February (~7.2°C). Because no short-term weather forecasting data is available for the dates in question (knowledge cutoff 2025-11-09), we must rely purely on comparing climatological distributions. The difference between the target date temperature and the November 9th temperature, T_target - T_Nov9, follows a normal distribution centered on the difference of their means. Due to the seasonal cooling down to winter, the difference in means is negative (approx -1.0°C for Nov 16, -3.1°C for Dec 9, and -3.6°C for Feb 7). Using a typical winter daily standard deviation of around 4°C for France, we can calculate the z-scores for the probability that the difference is greater than zero. These calculations result in approximately 42% for 1 week out, 29% for 1 month out, and 26% for 3 months out. The probabilities decrease further from 0.5 because the underlying seasonal expected value moves significantly further away from the November 9th baseline.
